extra.reasoning: The input variable `palpitations` does not appear in any rule. The seven rules reference only chest_pain, fever, slurred_speech, rash, focal_weakness, back_pain, and vomiting. Conflict resolution is per-output (highest-priority matching rule wins, ties by listed order), and the default acuity:=routine applies when no rule fires. Since palpitations is never read by any rule, the set of matching rules, their priorities, and therefore the computed acuity are entirely determined by the other seven inputs. Toggling palpitations from false to true (with all else fixed) neither adds nor removes any matching rule, so the resulting acuity is identical: acuity_after == acuity_before for every input. The property requires acuity_after >= acuity_before (no lower than before); equality satisfies this. Hence the monotonicity property holds across the entire input space.
